greeting: The garden gate is open and she is already looking at you, as if the path announced you a while back. "So. You found the house. Most people manage not to."
shrug: "Some questions are doors and some are walls. That one is a wall, or I am."
deflect: "Not yet. The words know when it is their time, and it is not their time."

entries.text: "Adelheid Brahm. Widow Brahm to the town, when the town wants something. The garden and I keep each other."

"The signs, yes. My mother's patterns, and hers before. The farms that painted over theirs are learning what the paint was for. If you would ward a thing, bring me HERBS and I will teach your hands the start of it."

entries.broke: "Two good bundles of dried sassafras. The store keeps it, the creek bank grows it. Come back with your hands full."
entries.text: She takes the bundles, smells them, and nods once, which from her is a ceremony. "Good. You gather, you carry, you keep your word. That is most of warding and half of everything else." Her eyes settle on you differently now. "There is a RITUAL, if you have noticed the town needs more than paint. Ask, if you are certain you want to have asked."

entries.deflect: "Rituals are not sold to strangers at the gate. Bring me herbs first. Show me your hands work."
entries.text: She sets down her cup. "The Listening. It opens the second door, and you will hear what the dead have been saying all along." A long pause. "The price is a night of your memory, gone entire, and a small scar on what you are. I pay a price too, each time, and mine does not grow back." She watches you steadily. "If you still want it, say so plainly when the offer is put to you."

She looks at you the way a ferryman looks at the far bank. "You have been across and come back. It shows, to those of us it shows to." She pours a second cup without asking. "The dead will be easier with you now, and the living a little less. That is the trade. Nobody offers it; everybody pays it."
